title: Resolve one active Downtown course through dependent register reads
capability_objective: Resolve this course from the noisy register: exact name Applied Microeconomics; exact location Downtown; required status active. Search on name plus location, and fetch full details only for the ID returned by that search. Treat all other records as distractors. No list, availability, profile, create, update, cancel, or notify calls. Finish with exactly three bullets: stable ID, status, and location/date.
prompt:
Resolve one course from the supplied sandboxed register. Work only in this workspace. Read `COURSE_REGISTER_GUIDE.md`, then inspect the built-in help of the local `./coursedesk` executable before accessing course data. Use Codex's genuine native execution tool to run the executable. The executable is the sole supported course-data interface. Do not inspect or parse the executable, protected files, the backing or runtime database, generated execution evidence, or the verifier, and do not query storage directly. Do not write a script, function, loop, wrapper, compound command, or persistent shell session to perform the lookup workflow.

The requested course has the exact name `Applied Microeconomics`, the exact location `Downtown`, and the required status `active`. The first course-register data operation must be one search jointly constrained to the exact requested name and location, made as the only register operation in that execution-tool action. Do not add a status constraint, broaden either value, list the register, or use an ID from the guide, filenames, memory, or any other source. Wait for the search response. Continue only if it contains exactly one match with one nonempty stable ID. Then, in a separate later execution-tool action, retrieve the full record for exactly that returned stable ID. Do not chain, combine, parallelize, or run search and retrieval in the same execution action. Do not retrieve any other ID. Confirm the required active status only from that successful full-record response. If the scoped search is missing, empty, ambiguous, or malformed, or if the full record is not active, stop unresolved without substituting another record or creating the deliverable.

This resolution is strictly read-only. Invoke no course-register operation except the one required search and the one eligible full-record retrieval. In particular, do not invoke list, availability, profile, create, update, cancel, or notify. Same-name courses elsewhere, similar names Downtown, stale terms, inactive records, unrelated courses, and visually similar identifiers are distractors and must not be inspected or mentioned. Apart from the runtime database and signed evidence created automatically by `coursedesk`, do not alter course, notification, runtime, journal, executable, guide, protected, or verifier state. Do not manually create, edit, replay, move, or delete execution evidence. Writing the required report is the only direct filesystem change permitted, and no scratch artifacts may remain.

After the successful full-record retrieval confirms active status, create `course-resolution.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines, with these labels and this order: `Stable ID`, `Status`, and `Location/date`. Use the form `- Label: value`. For the final bullet, combine the returned location and date with the exact separator ` / `. Populate every value only from the corresponding field in the successful full-record response, preserve returned strings exactly, and include no heading, blank line, code fence, table, or other prose. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the report. On success, make the final assistant response exactly the contents of `course-resolution.md`.
